zh.lifeSummary: 當Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex於1160年出生在英國英格蘭肯特托布利其時，她的父親Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford44歲，母親Matilda de Saint-Hilaire28歲。她於1184年1月25日與Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex在英國英格蘭艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1225年6月4日在英國英格蘭肯特托布利其托布利其 卡素享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex于1160年出生在英国英格兰肯特托布利其时，她的父亲Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford44岁，母亲Matilda de Saint-Hilaire28岁。她于1184年1月25日与Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex在英国英格兰艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1225年6月4日在英国英格兰肯特托布利其托布利其 卡素享年65岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Essex
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the county of Essex, whose name is from Old English ēast ‘east’ + Seaxe ‘Saxons’. In England, the surname is now particularly common in Birmingham.

nameMeanings.name: Aveline
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It seems to represent an Old French pet form of the Germanic name   Avila   (a derivative of   Avis  ), but it could also be an elaborated form of   Ava .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The normal English vernacular form of   Clara   during the Middle Ages and since.

en.lifeSummary: When Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex was born in 1160, in Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, was 44 and her mother, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, was 28. She married Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex on 25 January 1184, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 6 daughters. She died on 4 June 1225, in Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65, and was buried in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.of Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, En: Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex est née en 1160, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex le 25 janvier 1184, à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 4 juin 1225, à Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Towcester, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex 1160 in Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford 44 und ihre Mutter Matilda de Saint-Hilaire 28. Sie hat am 25. Januar 1184 in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, United Kingdom Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 4. Juni 1225 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex è nata il nel 1160 nel Tonbridge, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, aveva 44 anni e sua madre, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex il 25 gennaio 1184, nel Saffron Walden, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 6 figlie. Morì il 4 giugno 1225, nel Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolta a Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Aveline de Clare Countess of Essexが1160年にイギリスイギリスケントトンブリッジで生まれた時，父Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertfordは44歳，母Matilda de Saint-Hilaireは28歳であった。1184年1月25日にイギリスイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンでGeoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essexと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1225年6月4日にイギリスイギリスケントトンブリッジトンブリッジ カストルで65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアトウセスターにて埋葬された。

ko.of Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, En: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 토브리지 토브리지 카스틀
ko.lifeSummary: Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex(이)가 1160년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 토브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda de Saint-Hilaire(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1184년 1월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 사프런 월든에서 Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1225년 6월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 토브리지 토브리지 카스틀에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 타우스스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex nasceu em 1160, em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex em 25 de janeiro de 1184, em Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 4 de junho de 1225, em Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 65 anos, e foi sepultada em Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.of Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, En: тонбридж кастл, тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex родилась в 1160 г. в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex 25 января 1184 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 4 июня 1225 г. в местности тонбридж кастл, тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности тоусестир, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.of Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, En: Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Saffron Walden, Essex, England, United K: Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Aveline de Clare Countess of Essex nació en 1160, en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger de Clare 2nd Earl of Hertford, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Matilda de Saint-Hilaire, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Geoffrey I FitzPiers Earl of Essex el 25 de enero de 1184, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 6 hijas. Murió el 4 de junio de 1225, en Tonbridge Castle, Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultada en Towcester, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
